name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram operates a global network of agent locations offering money transfer, bill payment, and check cashing services through retail partners like CVS and Walmart.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer and financial services company that operates through a distributed network of agent locations rather than its own storefronts. The company facilitates both domestic and international money transfers, allowing customers to send and receive funds at thousands of partner locations worldwide. MoneyGram's business model relies on third-party retailers—primarily pharmacy chains like CVS and big-box retailers like Walmart—to serve as agents offering their services to consumers.

The company's primary services include money transfers (sending and receiving), check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment options. Customers can conduct transactions either online through MoneyGram's website or in person at physical agent locations. The Jacksonville, Florida search results show 63 locations within the greater Jacksonville area, with most partner locations offering extended hours (many open until 9 PM–midnight). The company provides a straightforward location finder tool allowing customers to search by address, city, state, or ZIP code.

MoneyGram distinguishes itself through its extensive retail partnership network, which provides convenience and accessibility compared to traditional banking channels. By operating through existing retail locations, the company offers customers the ability to access services at locations they already frequent for shopping. The multi-channel approach (online and offline) allows flexibility in how customers conduct their transactions. The widespread agent network and extended hours at many locations cater to customers who need financial services outside traditional banking hours.

MoneyGram's service is straightforward but comes with standard limitations of check-cashing and money transfer services. The website focuses primarily on location discovery and transaction management rather than detailed fee, rate, or service transparency. For consumers using MoneyGram, understanding specific fees, exchange rates (for international transfers), and processing times requires direct contact with agents or visit to the website's terms. The service is most practical for customers seeking quick access to money transfers or check cashing at convenient retail locations rather than those seeking financial products with built-in consumer protections or credit-building features.
pros: Extensive agent network with 63+ locations in Jacksonville area alone, providing high accessibility
Partner locations include major retailers (CVS, Walmart) with extended operating hours (many until 9 PM–midnight)
Dual-channel service model allows both online and offline transaction options
No requirement for bank account or credit history to use services
Quick transaction processing for money transfers and check cashing
Global money transfer capability through worldwide agent network
Simple location finder tool with multiple search methods (address, city, state, ZIP)
cons: Fee structure and exchange rates not transparently displayed on website—require direct inquiry with agents
Limited detail on processing times for different transaction types
Dependent on third-party retail partners; service hours vary by location
No credit-building or financial product benefits; purely transactional service
Website lacks information about consumer protections, dispute resolution, or service guarantees
best_for: Consumers without bank accounts seeking check cashing and money transfer services
International remittance senders needing quick transfer options at convenient retail locations
Individuals preferring offline transaction options at extended-hour retail locations
Customers seeking quick money transfers without credit checks or account requirements
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need quick access to check cashing, money transfers, or bill payment services at convenient retail locations without bank account requirements. The main caveat is that fees and exchange rates are not transparently published on their website, requiring direct contact with agent locations for pricing, and the service provides no credit-building benefits or long-term financial products.
services: In-person transaction processing at retail agent locations
